title: The Gang Turns Black

jokes.text: Well, it's Old Black Man's night to pick the movie.
jokes.setup: Character explains why they're watching The Wiz
jokes.punchline: Casually refers to someone as 'Old Black Man' as if it's a normal name
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing the gang's casual racism disguised as familiarity. Economical and shocking.

dialogue.speaker: Gang Member
dialogue.line: Well, it's Old Black Man's night to pick the movie.

jokes.text: So what? Doesn't mean I want to watch Orlando Bloom in Boyz n the Hood.
jokes.setup: Discussion about race-swapped movie remakes
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong actor reference (Orlando Bloom instead of any black actor)
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with the wrong actor choice revealing character's confusion about race and Hollywood.

dialogue.line: So what? Doesn't mean I want to watch Orlando Bloom in Boyz n the Hood.

jokes.text: I don't know why it took 'em so long to realize that their lives matter. I mean, they-they've always mattered, you know?
jokes.setup: Character trying to sound progressive about Black Lives Matter
jokes.punchline: Completely misses the point in the most tone-deaf way possible
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - character thinks he's being enlightened while being incredibly offensive.

dialogue.line: I don't know why it took 'em so long to realize that their lives matter. I mean, they-they've always mattered, you know?

jokes.text: Oh, my God. He's gone.
jokes.setup: Lightning strike, everyone checking if they're okay
jokes.punchline: Old Black Man has completely vanished without explanation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the magical realism premise, economical delivery.

dialogue.line: Oh, my God. He's gone.

jokes.text: Well, that's interesting.
jokes.setup: Characters discovering they appear black in their reflections
jokes.punchline: Massively underwhelming reaction to supernatural transformation
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement for such a massive development. Economy of language is excellent.

dialogue.line: Well, that's interesting.

jokes.text: Why are we black people? But only in our reflections.
jokes.setup: Trying to understand their transformation
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact observation of the impossible rules of their situation
jokes.explanation: Good setup of the episode's central absurd premise with appropriate confusion.

dialogue.line: Why are we black people? But only in our reflections.

jokes.text: Are we in a dream? Do we got, we got to, like, pinch ourselves or something?
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out what's happening to them
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the most cliched dream-testing method
jokes.explanation: Relies on tired trope but fits character's limited problem-solving abilities.

dialogue.line: Are we in a dream? Do we got, we got to, like, pinch ourselves or something?

jokes.text: Musical number begins with characters singing about their confusion
jokes.setup: Serious dramatic moment of discovery
jokes.punchline: Characters suddenly burst into elaborate musical theater
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about musical conventions while simultaneously embracing them - classic Sunny absurdism

dialogue.line: Musical number begins with characters singing about their confusion

jokes.text: The Hot Chick, The Change-Up, Vice Versa, All of Me, Like Father, Like Son, Dating the Enemy, A Boy Thing, A Girl Thing, 13 on 30, Detention, Face/Off
jokes.setup: Trying to identify what movie trope they're experiencing
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire listing of increasingly obscure body-swap movies in musical form
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing character's encyclopedic knowledge of terrible movies.

dialogue.line: The Hot Chick, The Change-Up, Vice Versa, All of Me, Like Father, Like Son, Dating the Enemy, A Boy Thing, A Girl Thing, 13 on 30, Detention, Face/Off

jokes.text: Actually, no, Face/Off wouldn't work, because that's a face-switching movie.
jokes.setup: Listing body-swap movies
jokes.punchline: Interrupting the musical flow to make another pedantic distinction
jokes.explanation: Another great character beat showing obsession with categorization even during magical singing.

dialogue.speaker: Gang Member
dialogue.line: Actually, no, Face/Off wouldn't work, because that's a face-switching movie.

jokes.text: I think we're in the respite of a musical, where people can get out the necessary information that doesn't fit into rhyme form.
jokes.setup: Characters have been singing and now speaking normally
jokes.punchline: Academic analysis of musical theater structure while trapped in one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on musical theater conventions while actively experiencing them.

dialogue.line: I think we're in the respite of a musical, where people can get out the necessary information that doesn't fit into rhyme form.

jokes.text: Oh, Christ, I'm singing again.
jokes.setup: Character starts singing involuntarily
jokes.punchline: Immediate frustrated awareness of losing control
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of language expressing frustration with the musical format.

dialogue.line: Oh, Christ, I'm singing again.

jokes.text: I'm sorry... Old Man. Just Old Man.
jokes.setup: Character catches himself saying 'Old Black Man'
jokes.punchline: Awkward overcorrection that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing character's failed attempt at political correctness after transformation.

dialogue.line: I'm sorry... Old Man. Just Old Man.

jokes.text: Don't force the Wiz thing.
jokes.setup: Another character singing about being in The Wiz
jokes.punchline: Blunt rejection of the musical's thematic direction
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about characters rejecting the episode's own premise.

dialogue.line: Don't force the Wiz thing.

jokes.text: I appear to be a guy named Reggie Williams.
jokes.setup: Checking wallet contents
jokes.punchline: Formal, detached way of describing identity theft/body swap
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing clinical approach to supernatural situation.

dialogue.line: I appear to be a guy named Reggie Williams.

jokes.text: Oh! Maybe the lesson we're supposed to learn is that black people are broke.
jokes.setup: Character finds empty pockets
jokes.punchline: Immediate jump to racist generalization from single data point
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing immediate leap to prejudice. Dark but earned.

dialogue.line: Oh! Maybe the lesson we're supposed to learn is that black people are broke.

jokes.text: Oh! Black people are rich. That's the lesson.
jokes.setup: Previous character found empty pockets
jokes.punchline: Other character finds money and makes opposite generalization
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how characters make sweeping judgments from tiny samples.

dialogue.line: Oh! Black people are rich. That's the lesson.

jokes.text: Oh, right, that's the lesson we have to learn: that black people will ruin your credit.
jokes.setup: Discussion about canceling credit cards
jokes.punchline: Another racist assumption disguised as lesson-learning
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the pattern of racist lesson-making from mundane events.

dialogue.line: Oh, right, that's the lesson we have to learn: that black people will ruin your credit.

jokes.text: Well, I think we just found out a new rule.
jokes.setup: Police arrive immediately when they try to get into car
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to obvious racial profiling
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about experiencing systemic racism firsthand, delivered with characteristic understatement.

dialogue.line: Well, I think we just found out a new rule.

jokes.text: When we turn white again, I'll stop paying the checks.
jokes.setup: Discussing putting Old Man in nursing home
jokes.punchline: Frank casually planning to abandon financial responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - casual sociopathy delivered matter-of-factly.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: When we turn white again, I'll stop paying the checks.

jokes.text: I'm gonna say the 'N' word.
jokes.setup: Frank listing words he wants to say as a black person
jokes.punchline: Escalation to the most taboo word with childlike excitement
jokes.explanation: Shocking character moment that perfectly captures Frank's inappropriate excitement about racial boundaries.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm gonna say the 'N' word.

jokes.text: Why are you looking for a chance to say it?
jokes.setup: Frank's excitement about saying racial slurs
jokes.punchline: Charlie's bewildered but logical question
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that highlights the absurdity of Frank's position.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Why are you looking for a chance to say it?

jokes.text: What's up, my niggas?
jokes.setup: Extended musical number about not saying the word
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately says it anyway
jokes.explanation: Shocking punchline that completely undermines the musical number's message. Pure Frank character work.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: What's up, my niggas?

jokes.text: Nigga, you talking about Old Black Man. He right over there laying.
jokes.setup: Mac awkwardly tries to describe Old Black Man without using his name
jokes.punchline: Street character immediately knows exactly who he means by the problematic name
jokes.explanation: Reveals that everyone knows him by this awful name, making Mac's PC awkwardness pointless

dialogue.speaker: Street Character
dialogue.line: Nigga, you talking about Old Black Man. He right over there laying.

jokes.text: How much does this guy sleep? Is he a cat?
jokes.setup: Finding Old Man asleep again
jokes.punchline: Charlie's innocent animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie moment - childlike observation delivered with complete sincerity.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: How much does this guy sleep? Is he a cat?

jokes.text: Oh, not like a gang-gang, like a... Oh, right... this must sound weird.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining he's in 'a gang' to social worker
jokes.punchline: Moment of self-awareness about how this sounds
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Charlie self-awareness about how his life sounds to outsiders.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, not like a gang-gang, like a... Oh, right... this must sound weird.

jokes.text: Guess I'm just another black kid who doesn't know his dad.
jokes.setup: Charlie singing about his life story
jokes.punchline: Ending song with racist stereotype applied to himself
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever - Charlie's life actually fits the stereotype, making the observation sting differently.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Guess I'm just another black kid who doesn't know his dad.

jokes.text: Oh, unless he knows his dad. Oh, shoot. That was racist.
jokes.setup: Social worker responding to Charlie's song about absent fathers
jokes.punchline: Immediate realization of saying something inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how racial assumptions slip out even from well-meaning people.

dialogue.speaker: Social Worker
dialogue.line: Oh, unless he knows his dad. Oh, shoot. That was racist.

jokes.text: Musical number about not making assumptions followed by making assumptions
jokes.setup: Characters sing about not drawing conclusions about black people
jokes.punchline: Immediately make wild positive assumptions (doctor, chef, fireman)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical musical number about liberal overcompensation and positive stereotyping

dialogue.line: Musical number about not making assumptions followed by making assumptions

jokes.text: Dude. That could be... He probably does have priors. You prob... Oh, no, see. No. Let's not do that.
jokes.setup: Character worried about having priors as black person
jokes.punchline: Catches himself making racist assumption mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Shows character's limited progress in avoiding racist thoughts

dialogue.line: Dude. That could be... He probably does have priors. You prob... Oh, no, see. No. Let's not do that.

jokes.text: We're church blacks! Church people.
jokes.setup: Police officer treating them respectfully
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining their respectability with awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing desperate attempt to maintain respectability with terrible word choice.

dialogue.line: We're church blacks! Church people.

jokes.text: We're their kids! Holy shit! We reunited them.
jokes.setup: Old Man greeting older black couple
jokes.punchline: Charlie's excited realization they've solved the wrong problem
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation moment with Charlie's characteristic misplaced enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We're their kids! Holy shit! We reunited them.

jokes.text: I thought you said Blacula, like a black vampire.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Scott Bakula
jokes.punchline: Misheard as 'Blacula' - actual blaxploitation movie
jokes.explanation: Good character mishearing that references actual blaxploitation film, showing their mindset.

dialogue.line: I thought you said Blacula, like a black vampire.

jokes.text: The show... I can't get away from it.
jokes.setup: Gang asking if he's doing Quantum Leap
jokes.punchline: Actor's exhausted response to being typecast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about actor Scott Bakula being trapped by his most famous role.

dialogue.speaker: Scott Bakula
dialogue.line: The show... I can't get away from it.

jokes.text: Your residuals ran out. I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Gang asking if he gets money from the show
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission of financial reality for TV actors
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest admission about the entertainment industry. Economy of language is perfect.

dialogue.line: Your residuals ran out. I'm sorry.

jokes.text: Don't steal our leap.
jokes.setup: Leaving Scott Bakula at nursing home
jokes.punchline: Warning against stealing their fictional plot resolution
jokes.explanation: Absurd warning that treats fictional concept as steal-able property. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Don't steal our leap.

jokes.text: I feel like that's the nicest the police have ever treated me.
jokes.setup: Being released from police custody politely
jokes.punchline: Admission that their normal treatment by police is terrible
jokes.explanation: Dark observation about police treatment that reveals character's usual experiences.

dialogue.line: I feel like that's the nicest the police have ever treated me.

jokes.text: Do you guys remember that electronics store on Market Street? The Wiz? It's called The Wiz!
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out how to get back to normal
jokes.punchline: Excited realization about literal connection to movie title
jokes.explanation: Standard revelation moment that advances plot but isn't particularly clever.

dialogue.line: Do you guys remember that electronics store on Market Street? The Wiz? It's called The Wiz!

jokes.text: There's no Wiz. It's just the name of the damn store.
jokes.setup: Gang asking to see 'the Wiz'
jokes.punchline: Blunt deflation of their magical thinking
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of their hopes with economical, frustrated delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Store Owner
dialogue.line: There's no Wiz. It's just the name of the damn store.

jokes.text: You'll see that we're white men.
jokes.setup: Singing to convince store owner to help them
jokes.punchline: Claiming their 'true' racial identity as justification
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment showing characters' belief that whiteness grants them special consideration.

dialogue.line: You'll see that we're white men.

jokes.text: 911? I'd like to report a disturbance.
jokes.setup: Gang singing about being white inside
jokes.punchline: Store owner immediately calling police after their song
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how their behavior appears to normal people.

dialogue.speaker: Store Owner
dialogue.line: 911? I'd like to report a disturbance.

jokes.text: He's got a gun!
jokes.setup: Charlie holding toy train from earlier scene
jokes.punchline: Police officer misidentifying toy as weapon
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that transforms innocent callback into police violence. Very dark but earned.

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: He's got a gun!

jokes.text: I had the craziest dream. You were there. You were there. You all were singing.
jokes.setup: Characters wake up, experience might have been a dream
jokes.punchline: Old Black Man describes the episode we just watched as his dream
jokes.explanation: Classic 'it was all a dream' reveal with meta-commentary on the episode's musical nature

dialogue.speaker: Old Black Man
dialogue.line: I had the craziest dream. You were there. You were there. You all were singing.

jokes.text: All right, well, that's great. We don't give a shit, 'cause we don't like hearing about people's dreams.
jokes.setup: Old Man trying to share his dream about the episode
jokes.punchline: Brutal dismissal of his attempt to discuss the meaning
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing complete lack of empathy and self-reflection.

dialogue.line: All right, well, that's great. We don't give a shit, 'cause we don't like hearing about people's dreams.

jokes.text: Yeah, the only thing we learned is that you have terrible taste in movies.
jokes.setup: Old Man suggesting they should learn something from the experience
jokes.punchline: Complete rejection of any meaningful lesson in favor of movie criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing characters learned absolutely nothing, bringing it back to the original complaint.

dialogue.line: Yeah, the only thing we learned is that you have terrible taste in movies.

jokes.text: What are the rules?
jokes.setup: Old Man starting to sing the recurring song
jokes.punchline: The infectious musical format claiming another victim
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the musical format spreading beyond the main characters.

dialogue.speaker: Old Man
dialogue.line: What are the rules?

tmdb_overview: After an electric heating blanket shorts out while the gang watches the Wiz they look in the mirror and realize they've turned black. They have to figure out how to get back to being themselves by going through all the classic body switch movie shenanigans they encounter.
